title: Evals Are Broken, Use Them Anyway — Ara Khan, Cline
summary: Ara Khan argues that benchmark scores are neither gospel nor vibes, but require nuanced interpretation. He presents a three-zone framework for analyzing eval failures: obvious bugs, model-specific nuances, and benchmark overfitting, and recommends using portfolio allocation of failures to identify true performance drivers rather than chasing benchmark numbers.
